chunk_summary: Anemia and iron deficiency are critical determinants of stunting, impairing growth through reduced appetite, fatigue, immune dysfunction, and neurodevelopmental risks. India's Anaemia Mukt Bharat strategy and WIFS guidelines provide lifecycle interventions targeting children, adolescents, and pregnant women to address anemia and its role in chronic undernutrition.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of anemia and iron deficiency in chronic growth faltering and stunting.
Learn the clinical management strategies for anemia, including diagnostic evaluations and dietary optimization.
Explore India's Anaemia Mukt Bharat and WIFS programs for lifecycle anemia control.
Identify barriers to adherence in anemia treatment and strategies to overcome them.
key_facts: Anemia reduces appetite, increases fatigue, and worsens neurodevelopmental vulnerability, sustaining stunting.
India’s Anaemia Mukt Bharat strategy targets children aged 6–59 months, school-age children, adolescents, and pregnant/lactating women.
Weekly Iron and Folic Acid Supplementation (WIFS) is linked to school-based and out-of-school adolescent platforms.
First-line anemia evaluation includes CBC with indices and ferritin, interpreted in the context of inflammation.
Helminth treatment should follow national schedules to address persistent infections contributing to anemia.
Missed doses and side effects are common barriers to anemia treatment adherence.
reference_quote: India’s AMB (Anaemia Mukt Bharat) strategy provides a lifecycle dosing platform that can be operationalized in pediatric notes. It targets children 6–59 months, school-age children, and adolescents, and also includes pregnant and lactating women to break the intergenerational cycle.
document_key_topics: Role of anemia and iron deficiency in stunting
Clinical management of anemia in pediatric and adolescent populations
Barriers to adherence in anemia treatment
